name: Ghidra Decompiler (headless)
description: Decompile a native binary (ELF/PE/Mach-O) with full Ghidra headless analysis — higher fidelity than native-decompile (rizin pdg) for hard targets. Runs analyzeHeadless with a bundled Ghidra script that decompiles every function to one C file. Static: analyses + decompiles, never runs the binary. Prereq-gated on Ghidra's analyzeHeadless (needs a JRE 17+).

prerequisites.install_hint: Install Ghidra (https://ghidra-sre.org) + a JRE 17+; put <ghidra>/support/analyzeHeadless on PATH (or set GHIDRA_HOME and the runner will find it).

safety.notes: Trusted analyzer (Ghidra) over an untrusted binary — full auto-analysis + decompilation, never execution/emulation. Runs in a throwaway project dir (deleted after), analysis timeout-bounded. Ghidra's analysis is heavier than rizin's; use for hard targets.

payload.note: BYO Ghidra on PATH (a large JVM app; not bundled). We DO bundle the Ghidra post-script (runtime/ghidra_decompile.py, Jython) that decompiles all functions. The heavy sibling of native-decompile: native-decompile (rizin pdg) is Ghidra's decompiler without a JVM for the common case; this is full Ghidra for hard targets. Dispatcher prereq gate = honest degradation.
